title: Sorting
description: Master qsort() with custom comparator functions — the standard way to sort arrays of ints, strings, and structs in C interviews.

sections.title: Sorting integers with qsort
sections.explanation: **qsort()** is C's built-in sort from `<stdlib.h>`. Signature: `qsort(base, count, size, comparator)`. The comparator takes two `const void*` pointers, casts them, and returns negative (a < b), zero (a == b), or positive (a > b). For ascending int sort: `*(int*)a - *(int*)b`.

exercise.prompt: Sort the array {7, 3, 9, 1, 5} in ascending order using qsort. Write the comparator function and print the sorted array space-separated.

validation.message: Use qsort with a comparator that casts void pointers to int pointers

exercise.hint: Comparator: return *(int*)a - *(int*)b for ascending. Pass sizeof(int) as element size.

sections.title: Descending sort
sections.explanation: To sort descending, reverse the comparator: return `*(int*)b - *(int*)a`. This is a common interview trick — just swap a and b in the subtraction. Be careful with integer overflow for very large values; use comparison operators instead of subtraction when needed.

exercise.prompt: Sort {4, 8, 2, 6, 10} in descending order using qsort with a descending comparator. Print the result.

validation.message: Swap a and b in the comparator for descending order

exercise.hint: For descending: return *(int*)b - *(int*)a — just reverse a and b from ascending.

sections.title: Sorting structs
sections.explanation: To sort an array of structs, write a comparator that casts to the struct pointer type and compares the desired field. This is how you sort edges by weight, intervals by start time, or any custom data in interviews.

exercise.prompt: Define a struct Pair with fields `a` and `b`. Sort an array of pairs by field `a` ascending. If `a` is equal, sort by `b` descending. Print each pair as "a b".

validation.message: Sort by a ascending, then b descending within same a
Use struct comparator with two-level comparison

exercise.hint: First compare px->a - py->a (ascending). If equal, compare py->b - px->b (descending).
